summary: Initial use of Continuous Positive Airway Pressure (CPAP) should be attempted for this neonate due to its effectiveness as an early intervention strategy; if there’s no response within expected timeframes or signs indicate worsening condition, immediate transition towards Mechanical Ventilation may become essential based upon current best practices outlined through research studies focusing on similar scenarios involving very low birthweight premature babies experiencing acute respiratory failure post-LSCS delivery. Agent1 concludes after carefully weighing these factors against established guidelines aimed primarily toward managing such critically ill newborns efficiently while minimizing risks associated directly linked complications from prolonged intubation procedures when non-invasive methods fail adequately addressing primary concerns related to oxygen saturation levels among other physiological
